item.title: Nevada GOP Gov. Lombardo projected to face Democrat Ford in highly competitive race
item.description: Democrats are aiming to unseat Nevada GOP Gov. Joe Lombardo in one of the country's most tightly contested gubernatorial contests this year.

item.title: U.S. conducts retaliatory strikes on Iran for downing of Apache helicopter
item.description: The two helicopter pilots were uninjured. After the retaliatory strikes were announced, President Trump said, "I believe the response should be very strong, very powerful."

item.title: Evette, Wilson head to runoff in primary for SC governor, CBS News projects
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace, who led a high-profile campaign after sparring with President Trump, did not advance.

item.title: Hilton to face Becerra in election for California governor, CBS News projects
item.description: Republican Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host and adviser to former British Prime Minister David Cameron, will advance to November's general election in the race to become California's next governor, CBS News projects.

item.title: GOP Rep. Valadao to face progressive Villegas in key California House district
item.description: Progressive Randy Villegas' win is an embarrassing defeat for establishment Democrats amid an intraparty feud about the party's future.

item.title: 6/9: CBS Evening News
item.description: A 19-year-old is convicted of murder after a track meet stabbing; the U.S. launches new strikes on Iran.

item.title: Graham Platner's race for Senate in Maine and more primaries to watch tonight
item.description: Maine Democrat Graham Platner clinched the nomination to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November.

item.title: Democrat Annie Andrews projected to face off against Sen. Lindsey Graham
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham has held the seat since 2003, and Democrats have an uphill battle in any attempt to unseat him.

item.title: Graham Platner to win Maine Democratic Senate primary and face Sen. Susan Collins
item.description: The primary victories set up a race that could be key to Democrats' hopes of winning control of the Senate.

item.title: Graham Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, CBS News projects | Special Report
item.description: CBS News projects that Graham Platner has won Maine's Democratic Senate primary, setting up a November battle with Sen. Susan Collins for her seat. Platner had faced a bevy of scandals in recent weeks. Jessi Mitchell anchored CBS News' special report.

item.title: The goals and challenges for Artemis III
item.description: Four astronauts are expected to launch into Earth orbit next year as part of NASA's goal to return to the lunar surface. Retired NASA astronaut Clayton Anderson joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the mission's goals and challenges.

item.title: Working remotely full-time can negatively impact mental health, research finds
item.description: New research suggests that working remotely full-time can negatively affect people's mental health, despite polls that show people are willing to get paid less for that flexibility. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has more.

item.title: What to know about the new Artemis III mission and crew
item.description: NASA on Tuesday named the four astronauts who will participate in Artemis III mission, they are expected to launch into Earth orbit next year to test rendezvous and docking procedures with moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin. CBS News space contributor Christian Davenport has more.

item.title: Political strategists on House-passed bill to fund ICE, Epstein assistant interview, more
item.description: Democratic strategist Kendra Barkoff Lamy and Republican strategist Sarah Chamberlain join "The Takeout" with their thoughts on some of the top political headlines of the day.

item.title: Hollywood-turned Broadway stars open up at the Tonys: "I'm really proud"
item.description: Hollywood stars like Daniel Radcliffe and Rachel Dratch took to the stage this Broadway season. CBS News' Taylor Masi spoke with some of the stars at the 2026 Tony Awards.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony sentenced to 35 years in murder of Austin Metcalf
item.description: Karmelo Anthony was found guilty earlier Tuesday after a trial that examined the confrontation leading to Austin Metcalf's death at a Frisco track meet.

item.title: Ken Calvert, Young Kim advance in Congressional CA-40 primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Republicans Rep. Ken Calvert and Rep. Young Kim have advanced to the November election for California's 40th Congressional District, CBS News projects.

item.title: 6/9: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Primary day wrapping up in Maine as voters grapple with Graham Platner's scandals; U.S. launches retaliatory strikes against Iran.

item.title: Trump stands by decision to name Pulte to intel role despite revolt in Congress
item.description: Bill Pulte will begin serving as acting director of national intelligence in a week-and-a-half, President Trump said, effectively standing by his decision to name the housing regulator and Trump loyalist.

item.title: New sunscreen option wins FDA approval after years of delay
item.description: A sunscreen ingredient that's been available in Europe, Japan and South Korea for years has finally been approved by the FDA for sale in the U.S.

item.title: Emotions high as jury finds Karmelo Anthony guilty of murder in fatal track meet stabbing
item.description: Emotions were high after a jury in Texas found 19-year-old Karmelo Anthony guilty of murder in the fatal stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a high school track meet last year. Immediately after the verdict, tension erupted outside the courthouse. Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Primary day wrapping up in Maine as voters grapple with Graham Platner's scandals
item.description: Democrats in Maine appear poised to officially choose Graham Platner as their nominee to take on Republican Susan Collins for her U.S. Senate seat. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns and Robert Costa have more.

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: U.S. launches self-defense strikes against Iran in response to chopper downing, CENTCOM says
item.description: The U.S. military says it began retaliatory strikes on Iran in response to the downing of an Apache helicopter. CBS News' Eleanor Watson has more.

item.title: What will FEMA 2.0 look like? Less aid and higher flood insurance, analysis warns
item.description: A new analysis warns a proposed FEMA overhaul by a Trump-appointed panel would limit access to disaster aid for survivors, shifting the burden to state and local agencies amid hurricane season.

item.title: Vance: Iran deal could happen in a week or months from now
item.description: In an interview airing on "CBS Sunday Morning," Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. will reach a deal with Iran before November's midterm elections.

item.title: Dozens of 7,000-year-old headless human skeletons found in ditch
item.description: Researchers have discovered dozens of headless human skeletons in a ditch in Slovakia, which they believe date back 7,000 years.

item.title: Many soccer fans excited about U.S. hosting World Cup, CBS News poll finds
item.description: The U.S. being a host country for the World Cup generates interest among fans, but less so among those who are not soccer fans.

item.title: Ukraine winning war with Russia, retired U.S. generals say
item.description: Several retired U.S. generals and the former director of a U.S. intelligence agency told CBS News they believe Ukraine now has the upper hand in the war with Russia.

item.title: Mass transit to be put to the test for World Cup host cities
item.description: Eleven U.S. cities will host hundreds of thousands of World Cup fans over the next few weeks.
